food_subgroup: Tofu and soy-based dishes with vegetables and mushrooms

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for braised vegetable beancurd with mushrooms; closest parent 'tofu dish' (FOODON_03315873) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers soybean curd/tofu as ingredient category; no specific code for braised tofu with mushroom dish exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for this prepared tofu-mushroom dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of braised vegetable beancurd with fresh mushrooms is approximately 300 g, comprising tofu pieces, mushrooms, and mixed vegetables in a savoury braising sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Tauhu goreng / braised tofu with vegetables — IMR Malaysia MyFCD composite; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central tofu and mushroom entries
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single MyFCD entry exactly matches home-style braised vegetable beancurd with fresh mushrooms. Values are estimated from MyFCD braised tofu entries and USDA FoodData Central entries for firm tofu (FDC ID 172475), shiitake mushrooms (FDC ID 169242), and mixed vegetables, weighted by typical recipe proportions (approx. 50% tofu, 30% mushrooms, 20% vegetables and sauce). Energy and macros cross-checked against HPB Singapore nutrient data for similar tofu-vegetable dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of tofu and soy-based foods — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for braised vegetable beancurd with fresh mushrooms. GI estimated at 32 based on firm tofu GI of approximately 15 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021) and the low-GI contribution of mushrooms and non-starchy vegetables. The braising sauce (soy sauce, oyster sauce, small amount of cornstarch) contributes minimal glycaemic load. Composite GI weighted by carbohydrate contribution of each component. GL per 300 g serving = (32 × 4.2 × 3) / 100 = 4.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: Each 300 g serving provides approximately 930 mg sodium, representing about 40% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content from tofu
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 32 and a glycemic load of 4.0 per serving, this dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the high soy protein content from tofu may additionally support glycaemic control and insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Favourable fat profile and soy isoflavones from tofu
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (0.6 g per 100 g) with a beneficial polyunsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio, and tofu provides soy isoflavones that may support LDL cholesterol reduction; however, the moderate sodium content warrants attention in patients with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetable Beancurd Braised with Fresh Mushrooms is a popular Chinese-Malaysian and Singaporean Zi Char dish consisting of firm tofu (beancurd) cut into cubes or slices, braised together with fresh mushrooms (commonly shiitake, oyster, or button mushrooms) and mixed non-starchy vegetables such as bok choy, cabbage, or carrots in a savoury sauce made from soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, ginger, and a small amount of cornstarch for thickening. The dish is typically finished with a drizzle of sesame oil. The texture is soft and silky from the tofu, with an earthy umami depth from the mushrooms and a glossy, lightly thickened sauce. It is a staple of Zi Char (home-style Chinese restaurant cooking) menus across Malaysia and Singapore and is commonly ordered as a shared dish alongside rice. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-calorie, high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish with a low glycemic index of approximately 32, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels. The tofu provides plant-based protein (approximately 5.8 g per 100 g), calcium, and soy isoflavones. The main nutritional concern is sodium from the braising sauce, which can reach 310 mg per 100 g. The dish is naturally cholesterol-free and dairy-free, and is suitable for vegetarians when oyster sauce is substituted with vegetarian oyster sauce. It is classified as minimally processed and is a nutritionally balanced option within a Zi Char meal.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exactly matches home-style braised vegetable beancurd with fresh mushrooms as served in Malaysian/Singaporean Zi Char restaurants. Nutrient values are estimated from IMR Malaysia MyFCD braised tofu entries and USDA FoodData Central component data for firm tofu and mushrooms, weighted by typical recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on tofu GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central component data; no exact dish-level entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. Sodium content (310 mg per 100 g) is estimated based on typical Zi Char braising sauce proportions and may vary significantly depending on the amount of soy sauce and oyster sauce used. 3. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate based on firm tofu GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD or USDA component sources used for this estimation. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as tofu and fresh mushrooms contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central entries for firm tofu and shiitake mushrooms. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish containing no animal-derived ingredients in its standard form; naturally zero. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero. 8. The dish is labelled 'home-made' in the field survey listing but is served in a restaurant context (Marina Seafood House, Boat Quay); nutritional values reflect a typical restaurant preparation. 9. Dietary flag is_vegan is false because standard oyster sauce contains oyster extract; a vegan variant using vegetarian oyster sauce would qualify as vegan. 10. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
